note_data.title: Ratio and Proportion
keyPoints: Ratio is the comparison of two quantities of the same kind expressed as a:b or a/b
Proportion exists when two ratios are equal, written as a:b = c:d or a:b :: c:d
In proportion a:b :: c:d, terms a and d are called extremes, b and c are called means
Product of means equals product of extremes: b × c = a × d
Fourth proportional to a, b, c is d where a:b :: c:d
Third proportional to a, b is c where a:b :: b:c
Mean proportional between a and c is b where a:b :: b:c, so b = √(ac)
Direct proportion: when one quantity increases, other also increases proportionally
Inverse proportion: when one quantity increases, other decreases proportionally

definitions.definition: Ratio is the comparison between two quantities of the same kind. It is expressed as a:b or a/b where b ≠ 0.
An equality of two ratios is called proportion. If a:b = c:d, then a, b, c, d are said to be in proportion.
If three quantities a, b, c are given, then fourth proportional d is such that a:b :: c:d.
Mean proportional between two quantities a and c is b such that a:b :: b:c, where b = √(ac).
Two quantities are in direct proportion if increase in one quantity causes proportional increase in the other quantity.

importantQuestions.question: Define proportion and write its fundamental property.
importantQuestions.answer: Proportion is equality of two ratios. If a:b :: c:d, then fundamental property is: product of means = product of extremes, i.e., b × c = a × d.

importantQuestions.question: The monthly salary of three employees in Karachi are in the ratio 5:7:9. If the total salary is Rs. 63,000, find individual salaries. Also find the mean proportional between the first and third salary.
importantQuestions.answer: Let salaries be 5x, 7x, 9x. Total = 5x + 7x + 9x = 21x = 63,000. So x = 3,000. First salary = Rs. 15,000, Second = Rs. 21,000, Third = Rs. 27,000. Mean proportional between 15,000 and 27,000 = √(15,000 × 27,000) = √405,000,000 = Rs. 20,124.6

mcqs.question: If a:b :: c:d, then which property holds?
If 3:4 :: x:12, then value of x is:

commonMistakes: Students confuse mean proportional formula: it's √(ac), not (a+c)/2
In proportion problems, always check units are same before comparing ratios
When finding fourth proportional, maintain correct order: a:b :: c:d
Students forget to simplify ratios to lowest terms
In word problems, students don't clearly define what x represents
examTips: This chapter usually has 1-2 MCQs and 1 short question worth 3 marks in BSEK papers
Word problems on salaries, ages, and measurements appear every year
Always write 'Let the quantities be...' when solving ratio problems
Mean proportional questions are very common - memorize the formula b = √(ac)
formulas: Fourth proportional: a:b :: c:d, find d = (bc)/a
Third proportional: a:b :: b:c, find c = b²/a
Mean proportional between a and c: b = √(ac)
note_data.keyDiagram: No specific diagram required for this chapter, but students should know how to represent ratios using bar diagrams or line segments to show proportional parts visually.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — asked students to define ratio and proportion with one example each
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q7 (6 marks) — required solving word problems on direct and inverse proportion
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q4 (3 marks) — tested fourth proportional and mean proportional definitions
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q8 (5 marks) — combined ratio properties with practical applications in daily life

workedExample.question: If a:b = 3:4 and b:c = 2:5, find a:b:c. Also find the fourth proportional to 6, 9, and 8. (6 marks)
steps: Step 1: Given a:b = 3:4 and b:c = 2:5, make b equal in both ratios
Step 2: LCM of 4 and 2 is 4, so multiply second ratio by 2
Step 3: a:b = 3:4 and b:c = 4:10, therefore a:b:c = 3:4:10
Step 4: For fourth proportional, let x be the fourth proportional to 6, 9, 8
workedExample.markScheme: 2 marks for correct ratio calculation, 1 mark for LCM method, 2 marks for fourth proportional setup, 1 mark for final answer

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for ratio and proportion definitions.
Never confuse fourth proportional with mean proportional - fourth proportional involves four terms while mean proportional involves three terms only.
In compound ratio problems, students forget to make the common term equal before combining ratios, leading to incorrect final answers.
note_data.quickRecap: Ratio compares two quantities of same kind using division (a:b). Proportion states equality of two ratios (a:b = c:d). Fourth proportional to a,b,c is d where a:b = c:d. Mean proportional between a and c is b where a:b = b:c.
importantFormulas: If a:b = c:d, then ad = bc (cross multiplication property)
Fourth proportional: If a:b = c:x, then x = bc/a
Mean proportional: If a:x = x:b, then x = √(ab)
Compound ratio of a:b and c:d is ac:bd
